name: Expression Calculator
description: Build an expression calculator as two separate modules. Module tokenizer.py must implement: TokenType enum (NUMBER, PLUS, MINUS, MULTIPLY, DIVIDE, LPAREN, RPAREN, EOF), Token dataclass (type, value), and Tokenizer class that takes a string and produces a list of Tokens via tokenize() method. Module evaluator.py must import from tokenizer.py and implement ExpressionCalculator class with evaluate(expr: str) -> int method. The calculator must support: addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), integer division (//), parentheses for grouping, and unary minus. Operator precedence must be respected: parentheses > unary minus > multiplication/division > addition/subtraction.
signature:
# tokenizer.py
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass

class TokenType(Enum):
    NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
    PLUS = 'PLUS'
    MINUS = 'MINUS'
    MULTIPLY = 'MULTIPLY'
    DIVIDE = 'DIVIDE'
    LPAREN = 'LPAREN'
    RPAREN = 'RPAREN'
    EOF = 'EOF'

@dataclass
class Token:
    type: TokenType
    value: str | int

class Tokenizer:
    def __init__(self, text: str): ...
    def tokenize(self) -> list[Token]: ...

# evaluator.py
from tokenizer import Tokenizer, Token, TokenType

class ExpressionCalculator:
    def evaluate(self, expr: str) -> int: ...
constraints: Expression contains only digits, +, -, *, //, (, ), and spaces
All intermediate and final results fit in a 64-bit integer
Division is integer division (truncated toward zero)
tokenizer.py must export Tokenizer, Token, and TokenType
evaluator.py must import from tokenizer.py and export ExpressionCalculator
